trans-unit.note: Bundle display name
Bundle name
Copyright (human-readable)

trans-unit.target: Caker need SSH access to running VM and create virtual network

vide
A label displaying the name of a network. The argument is the name of the network.
A menu item that opens the about panel.
A button to add a new item.
Title of an alert when the user doesn't have admin rights.
A label for the administrator group.

trans-unit.id: Agent not installed. Please install the agent first.
trans-unit.source: Agent not installed. Please install the agent first.
trans-unit.target: Agent not installed. Please install the agent first.
trans-unit.note: Text displayed in a label view when the agent is not installed.

trans-unit.id: Allow to install privileged bootstrap files
trans-unit.source: Allow to install privileged bootstrap files
trans-unit.target: Allow to install privileged bootstrap files
trans-unit.note: Prompt displayed in a notification when requesting admin authorization.
Title of an alert dialog when an error occurs.
A label for the application settings tab.

trans-unit.id: Are you sure you want to delete %@? This action cannot be undone.
trans-unit.source: Are you sure you want to delete %@? This action cannot be undone.
trans-unit.target: Are you sure you want to delete %@? This action cannot be undone.
trans-unit.note: Text displayed in a dialog box that asks the user to confirm that they want to delete a virtual machine. The argument is the name of the virtual machine.

trans-unit.id: Are you sure you want to delete network %@? This action cannot be undone.
trans-unit.source: Are you sure you want to delete network %@? This action cannot be undone.
trans-unit.target: Are you sure you want to delete network %@? This action cannot be undone.

trans-unit.id: Are you sure you want to delete remote %@? This action cannot be undone.

trans-unit.target: Are you sure you want to delete remote %@? This action cannot be undone.

trans-unit.id: Are you sure you want to delete template %@? This action cannot be undone.
trans-unit.source: Are you sure you want to delete template %@? This action cannot be undone.
trans-unit.target: Are you sure you want to delete template %@? This action cannot be undone.
trans-unit.note: A description of an action that deletes a template.
A description of an error that might occur when executing a command with elevated privileges.
A description of an error that might occur when requesting admin authorization.
A toggle that enables or disables the autostart feature.
Error message when the build process fails.
A section for the CPU and memory settings of a virtual machine.
A section of the wizard for selecting the CPU count, memory size, and disk size.
Title of the CPU & Ram step in the wizard.
A label for the number of CPU cores.
A label displayed above a dropdown menu that lets the user select the cache mode of a disk attachment.
Error message when the path to the Caked bundle is missing.
Error message when the Caked executable is missing.
A button to cancel the current action.
Title of an alert when the user cancels the creation of a virtual machine.
Title of the alert when the certificates installation fails.
A button that opens a font panel to change the terminal font.
A button that checks for updates when pressed.
Title of the OS selection step in the wizard.
Title of the category for cloud images.
A label for a color picker.
Text displayed in a label view when connecting to a VNC.
A menu that allows the user to control the virtual machine.
A button that creates a new network.
A window title for the new document wizard.
A button that creates a new virtual machine.
Error message when the virtual machine cannot be created.
Error message when deleting a virtual machine fails.
Title of an alert that deletes a network.
Confirmation dialog title for deleting a remote.
Title of an alert that asks the user to confirm the deletion of a template.
The label of a button that shows the detail of a selected item
A section for sharing directories between the host and the guest.
Title of a wizard item for configuring the disk settings of a virtual machine.
A label for the path to the disk image.
A label for the disk path.
A label displayed in a row with a stepper.
A section for the disks attached to a virtual machine.
A
Title of an alert that appears when the user wants to duplicate a virtual machine.
A toggle that enables dynamic port forwarding.
A toggle to enable or disable VM screenshoting.
Label for the file name field in the save file dialog.
A heading for a sidebar.
Error message displayed when creating a template fails.
Error message when the VM URL is invalid.

trans-unit.id: Failed to ensure privileged bootstrap files

A button that opens a font panel to change the font of the terminal.
A description of an error that might occur when creating a network. The argument is the gateway address.
A label for the guest group ID.
A label for the user ID to use when mounting a folder.
A description of a template.
A label displayed in the detail view of a template.
A description of a view that displays a virtual machine.
A label displayed in the default case of a switch statement.

A window that displays the user's home screen.
A button that installs the agent.
A button that installs a system service for Caker.
Text displayed when the agent is being installed.
Error message when the VM URL is not a file URL.
Error message when an unexpected error occurs while creating a virtual machine.

A checkbox that allows the user to enable nested virtualization.
Title of a wizard item for configuring the network settings of the virtual machine.
A description of an error that might occur when creating a network.
A section for configuring the network attachements of a virtual machine.
Title of the category for the list of networks.
A section for creating a new disk attachment.

A section for creating a new port forwarding.
A section for creating a new socket endpoint.
A button that opens a dialog to create a new virtual machine.
A button to advance to the next step.
Error message when no VM is found.
A message displayed when there are no virtual machines available.
A button that opens a new virtual machine.
A menu with options.
A button that adds something.
Title of a port forwarding tunnel.
Title of a wizard item for configuring the ports of the virtual machine.
A button to go back.
A button that quits the application.
A toggle that allows or disables writing to the disk.
A checkbox that allows the user to enable the refitting of the display.
A button that removes the Caked service.

A toggle to enable or disable saving VM screenshots.
A checkbox to select an item.

trans-unit.id: This option allow display window of running vm to debug it
trans-unit.source: This option allow display window of running vm to debug it
trans-unit.target: This option allow display window of running vm to debug it

trans-unit.id: This option allow mode to connect to a VMRun service endpoint
trans-unit.source: This option allow mode to connect to a VMRun service endpoint
trans-unit.target: This option allow mode to connect to a VMRun service endpoint

trans-unit.id: This option allow mode to connect to a running service
trans-unit.source: This option allow mode to connect to a running service
trans-unit.target: This option allow mode to connect to a running service

trans-unit.id: This option allow run vm in service mode
trans-unit.source: This option allow run vm in service mode
trans-unit.target: This option allow run vm in service mode

trans-unit.id: This option allow run vnc server with custom port
trans-unit.source: This option allow run vnc server with custom port
trans-unit.target: This option allow run vnc server with custom port

trans-unit.id: This option allow run vnc server with password
trans-unit.source: This option allow run vnc server with password
trans-unit.target: This option allow run vnc server with password

trans-unit.id: This option tell that vm run from service
trans-unit.source: This option tell that vm run from service
trans-unit.target: This option tell that vm run from service

trans-unit.id: Using this argument tell caked to act as system agent, which means it will run as a daemon. This option is useful when you want to run caked as a launchd service
trans-unit.source: Using this argument tell caked to act as system agent, which means it will run as a daemon. This option is useful when you want to run caked as a launchd service
trans-unit.target: Using this argument tell caked to act as system agent, which means it will run as a daemon. This option is useful when you want to run caked as a launchd service

trans-unit.id: You can only specify one of --network or --pidfile
trans-unit.source: You can only specify one of --network or --pidfile
trans-unit.target: You can only specify one of --network or --pidfile

trans-unit.id: You must specify one of --network or --pidfile
trans-unit.source: You must specify one of --network or --pidfile
trans-unit.target: You must specify one of --network or --pidfile

trans-unit.id: dhcp-end is not a valid IP address
trans-unit.source: dhcp-end is not a valid IP address
trans-unit.target: dhcp-end is not a valid IP address

trans-unit.id: dhcp-end is not allowed for bridged mode
trans-unit.source: dhcp-end is not allowed for bridged mode
trans-unit.target: dhcp-end is not allowed for bridged mode

trans-unit.id: dhcp-end is not in the same network as gateway
trans-unit.source: dhcp-end is not in the same network as gateway
trans-unit.target: dhcp-end is not in the same network as gateway

trans-unit.id: dhcp-end is required for host/shared mode when gateway is specified
trans-unit.source: dhcp-end is required for host/shared mode when gateway is specified
trans-unit.target: dhcp-end is required for host/shared mode when gateway is specified

trans-unit.id: fd and socket-path are mutually exclusive %lld %@

trans-unit.target: fd and socket-path are mutually exclusive %1$lld %2$@

trans-unit.id: gateway is not a valid IP address
trans-unit.source: gateway is not a valid IP address
trans-unit.target: gateway is not a valid IP address

trans-unit.id: gateway is not allowed for bridged mode
trans-unit.source: gateway is not allowed for bridged mode
trans-unit.target: gateway is not allowed for bridged mode

trans-unit.id: gateway used for --vmnet=(host|shared), e.g., "192.168.105.1" (default: decided by macOS)
trans-unit.source: gateway used for --vmnet=(host|shared), e.g., "192.168.105.1" (default: decided by macOS)
trans-unit.target: gateway used for --vmnet=(host|shared), e.g., "192.168.105.1" (default: decided by macOS)

trans-unit.id: interface is required for bridged mode
trans-unit.source: interface is required for bridged mode
trans-unit.target: interface is required for bridged mode

trans-unit.id: pidfile is required when using fd
trans-unit.source: pidfile is required when using fd
trans-unit.target: pidfile is required when using fd

trans-unit.id: pidfile is required when using vmfd
trans-unit.source: pidfile is required when using vmfd
trans-unit.target: pidfile is required when using vmfd

trans-unit.id: valid netmask is required for host/shared mode when gateway is specified
trans-unit.source: valid netmask is required for host/shared mode when gateway is specified
trans-unit.target: valid netmask is required for host/shared mode when gateway is specified

trans-unit.id: %@ already in use, try umounting it
trans-unit.source: %@ already in use, try umounting it
trans-unit.target: %@ already in use, try umounting it

trans-unit.id: %@ permission denied, consider changing the disk's owner using "sudo chown $USER %@" or run as a superuser
trans-unit.source: %1$@ permission denied, consider changing the disk's owner using "sudo chown $USER %2$@" or run as a superuser
trans-unit.target: %1$@ permission denied, consider changing the disk's owner using "sudo chown $USER %2$@" or run as a superuser

trans-unit.id: Add a network interface to the instance
trans-unit.source: Add a network interface to the instance
trans-unit.target: Add a network interface to the instance

trans-unit.id: Allow to create virtio socket between guest and host, format like url: <bind|connect|tcp|udp>://<address>:<port number>/<file for unix socket>, eg. bind://dummy:1234/tmp/vsock.sock
trans-unit.source: Allow to create virtio socket between guest and host, format like url: <bind|connect|tcp|udp>://<address>:<port number>/<file for unix socket>, eg. bind://dummy:1234/tmp/vsock.sock
trans-unit.target: Allow to create virtio socket between guest and host, format like url: <bind|connect|tcp|udp>://<address>:<port number>/<file for unix socket>, eg. bind://dummy:1234/tmp/vsock.sock

trans-unit.id: Allow to use dynamic port forwarding, default is false
trans-unit.source: Allow to use dynamic port forwarding, default is false
trans-unit.target: Allow to use dynamic port forwarding, default is false

trans-unit.id: Attaching Network Block Devices are not supported prior MacOS 14
trans-unit.source: Attaching Network Block Devices are not supported prior MacOS 14
trans-unit.target: Attaching Network Block Devices are not supported prior MacOS 14

trans-unit.id: By default monolithic method is used for uploading blobs to the registry but some registries support a more efficient chunked method. For example, AWS Elastic Container Registry supports only chunks larger than 5MB but GitHub Container Registry supports only chunks smaller than 4MB. Google Container Registry on the other hand doesn't support chunked uploads at all. Please refer to the documentation of your particular registry in order to see if this option is suitable for you and what's the recommended chunk size.
trans-unit.source: By default monolithic method is used for uploading blobs to the registry but some registries support a more efficient chunked method. For example, AWS Elastic Container Registry supports only chunks larger than 5MB but GitHub Container Registry supports only chunks smaller than 4MB. Google Container Registry on the other hand doesn't support chunked uploads at all. Please refer to the documentation of your particular registry in order to see if this option is suitable for you and what's the recommended chunk size.
trans-unit.target: By default monolithic method is used for uploading blobs to the registry but some registries support a more efficient chunked method. For example, AWS Elastic Container Registry supports only chunks larger than 5MB but GitHub Container Registry supports only chunks smaller than 4MB. Google Container Registry on the other hand doesn't support chunked uploads at all. Please refer to the documentation of your particular registry in order to see if this option is suitable for you and what's the recommended chunk size.

trans-unit.id: Chunk size in MB if registry supports chunked uploads
trans-unit.source: Chunk size in MB if registry supports chunked uploads
trans-unit.target: Chunk size in MB if registry supports chunked uploads

trans-unit.target: Clone a VM from a registry

trans-unit.target: Disables audio and entropy devices and switches to only Mac-specific input devices.

trans-unit.target: Duplicate a VM to a new name

trans-unit.target: Entries to remove: "caches" targets caches and "vms" targets local VMs or boths.

trans-unit.id: File descriptor is not valid. error: %@

trans-unit.target: File descriptor is not valid. error: %@

trans-unit.id: First ip used for the configured shared network, e.g., "192.168.105.1"

trans-unit.target: First ip used for the configured shared network, e.g., "192.168.105.1"

trans-unit.id: For example, --older-than=7 will remove entries that weren't accessed by caked in the last 7 days.
trans-unit.source: For example, --older-than=7 will remove entries that weren't accessed by caked in the last 7 days.
trans-unit.target: For example, --older-than=7 will remove entries that weren't accessed by caked in the last 7 days.

trans-unit.id: For example, --space-budget=50 will effectively shrink all entries to a total size of 50 gigabytes.
trans-unit.source: For example, --space-budget=50 will effectively shrink all entries to a total size of 50 gigabytes.
trans-unit.target: For example, --space-budget=50 will effectively shrink all entries to a total size of 50 gigabytes.

trans-unit.target: Invalid console URL: file descriptor %@ is not a number

trans-unit.target: Invalid console URL: file descriptor %1$d is not valid. error: %2$@

trans-unit.id: Invalid console URL: file descriptor is not specified
trans-unit.source: Invalid console URL: file descriptor is not specified
trans-unit.target: Invalid console URL: file descriptor is not specified
